---
title: "Lab 5: Browser Information, Objects, and Script Reuse"
date: "2026-02-20"
due: "2026-02-25"
---


This lab covers workbook experiments 9, 10, and 11.

## Workbook Alignment

- Experiment 9: Display browser information
- Experiment 10: Merge properties of two objects
- Experiment 11: Include one JavaScript file into another

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this lab, you will be able to:

- read browser details using the `navigator` object
- create and merge JavaScript objects
- organize reusable code across multiple JavaScript files
- control script loading order in HTML
- display dynamic output inside the page

## Software and Files Required

- completion of Lab 4
- VS Code or any text editor
- a modern browser with developer tools
- a folder named `Lab05`
- files named `browser-info.html`, `merge-objects.html`, `index.html`, `math-utils.js`, and `app.js`

## Experiment 9: Display Browser Information

### Problem Statement

Create a web page that displays browser details using JavaScript.

### Folder Structure

```text
Lab05/
  browser-info.html
  merge-objects.html
  index.html
  math-utils.js
  app.js
```

### Procedure

1. Create the `Lab05` folder.
2. Add a new file named `browser-info.html`.
3. Access the `navigator` object using JavaScript.
4. Store key browser properties in an object.
5. Display the properties as list items in the browser.

### Solution Code

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Browser Information</title>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Browser Information</h1>
  <ul id="browserInfo"></ul>

  <script>
    const info = {
      appName: navigator.appName,
      appVersion: navigator.appVersion,
      platform: navigator.platform,
      language: navigator.language,
      userAgent: navigator.userAgent
    };

    const list = document.getElementById("browserInfo");
    Object.entries(info).forEach(([key, value]) => {
      const item = document.createElement("li");
      item.textContent = key + ": " + value;
      list.appendChild(item);
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Expected Result

The page should display browser name, version, platform, language, and user agent information as a list.

## Experiment 10: Merge Properties of Two Objects

### Problem Statement

Write a JavaScript program to merge the properties of two objects and display the combined result.

### Procedure

1. Create a file named `merge-objects.html`.
2. Define two objects with related data.
3. Merge them using the spread operator.
4. Display the merged object using formatted JSON output.
5. Test the page in the browser.

### Solution Code

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Merge Objects</title>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Merge Two Objects</h1>
  <pre id="mergedOutput"></pre>

  <script>
    const student = {
      name: "Neha",
      rollNumber: "MCA24012"
    };

    const academic = {
      course: "BMC201",
      semester: 2
    };

    const merged = { ...student, ...academic };
    document.getElementById("mergedOutput").textContent = JSON.stringify(merged, null, 2);
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Expected Result

The page should display a single object containing all properties from both source objects.

## Experiment 11: Include One JavaScript File into Another Page

### Problem Statement

Create an HTML page that uses functions defined in a separate JavaScript file.

### Procedure

1. Create the files `math-utils.js`, `app.js`, and `index.html`.
2. Define reusable functions in `math-utils.js`.
3. Write page logic in `app.js`.
4. Load both files in the correct order from `index.html`.
5. Open the page and verify that both functions are available.

### `math-utils.js`

```javascript
function add(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

function multiply(a, b) {
  return a * b;
}
```

### `app.js`

```javascript
window.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
  const output = document.getElementById("scriptOutput");
  output.textContent = "Sum: " + add(10, 20) + " | Product: " + multiply(10, 20);
});
```

### `index.html`

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Script Reuse</title>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Including JavaScript Files</h1>
  <p id="scriptOutput"></p>

  <script src="math-utils.js"></script>
  <script src="app.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Expected Result

The page should display the calculated sum and product, proving that functions defined in one script file can be reused in another page script.

## Observation Questions

1. What is the purpose of the `navigator` object?
2. What happens when two merged objects have the same property name?
3. Why should utility scripts be loaded before dependent scripts?
4. What is the difference between `DOMContentLoaded` and `load`?

## Viva Questions

1. What is the advantage of separating utility code into another file?
2. What is the role of `JSON.stringify()` in this lab?
3. Why is script order important in HTML?
4. How can browser developer tools help debug JavaScript loading issues?

## Submission Checklist

- `browser-info.html`
- `merge-objects.html`
- `index.html`
- `math-utils.js`
- `app.js`
- screenshots of all three pages
- short answer file for observation and viva questions

## Extension Task

Add another utility function, such as `subtract()` or `divide()`, and update the page so it displays all available calculations.
